error_signature: client IP address not found in webhook node
title: Client IP address not available in Webhook node
symptom: The Webhook node does not expose the client IP address directly, leading to difficulties in fetching it.
applies_when: Running in a production/behind proxy environment
not_this_if: Testing locally without a reverse proxy
Client IP is sent in the request body
root_cause: The Webhook node in n8n does not include the client IP in the default JSON body. Instead, it relies on headers like 'x-forwarded-for' or 'x-real-ip' to retrieve the client IP, which may not be configured correctly in some environments.
fix_steps: Add a Set or Function node after the Webhook node.
Access the client IP using the expression: {{$json[“headers”][“x-forwarded-for”] || $json[“headers”][“x-real-ip”]}}.
If testing locally, consider fetching the client IP from an external service and include it in the payload.
agent_instruction: Use a Set or Function node to extract the client IP from the headers in the Webhook node.
safety_notes: Sending the IP in the body is for logging purposes only and should not be relied upon for security.
